issues.code:
4 import pickle
5 import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET
6
issues.description: Using xml.etree.ElementTree to parse untrusted XML data is known to be vulnerable to XML attacks. Replace xml.etree.ElementTree with the equivalent defusedxml package, or make sure defusedxml.defuse_stdlib() is called.

issues.code:
17                 settings_bytes = base64.b64decode(settings_base64)
18                 settings = pickle.loads(settings_bytes)
19                 if not isinstance(settings, dict) or not all(isinstance(k, str) and isinstance(v, (str, int, float, bool)) for k, v in settings.items()):
issues.description: Pickle and modules that wrap it can be unsafe when used to deserialize untrusted data, possible security issue.

Using xml.etree.ElementTree.fromstring to parse untrusted XML data is known to be vulnerable to XML attacks. Replace xml.etree.ElementTree.fromstring with its defusedxml equivalent function or make sure defusedxml.defuse_stdlib() is called
Pickle deserialization of untrusted data can lead to code execution; avoid pickle for untrusted inputs.
XML parsing depends on a [user-provided value](1) without guarding against uncontrolled entity expansion.
Unsafe deserialization depends on a [user-provided value](1).

issues.code:
3 import base64
4 import pickle
5 import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET
issues.description: Consider possible security implications associated with pickle module.
issues.judge_reason: Importing pickle itself is flagged as a risk. While the import alone is not exploitable, it is used unsafely at line 18. This is a low-severity informational finding that supports the B301 finding.

issues.code:
4 import pickle
5 import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET
6
issues.description: Using xml.etree.ElementTree to parse untrusted XML data is known to be vulnerable to XML attacks. Replace xml.etree.ElementTree with the equivalent defusedxml package, or make sure defusedxml.defuse_stdlib() is called.
issues.judge_reason: Importing xml.etree.ElementTree is flagged as a risk. The import itself is not directly exploitable, but it is used unsafely at line 33. This is a low-severity informational finding supporting B314.

issues.code:
17                 settings_bytes = base64.b64decode(settings_base64)
18                 settings = pickle.loads(settings_bytes)
19                 if not isinstance(settings, dict) or not all(isinstance(k, str) and isinstance(v, (str, int, float, bool)) for k, v in settings.items()):
issues.description: Pickle and modules that wrap it can be unsafe when used to deserialize untrusted data, possible security issue.
issues.judge_reason: pickle.loads() is called on user-supplied base64-decoded data without any sanitization. The code only validates the resulting type after deserialization, which does not prevent arbitrary code execution during unpickling.

issues.description: Using xml.etree.ElementTree.fromstring to parse untrusted XML data is known to be vulnerable to XML attacks. Replace xml.etree.ElementTree.fromstring with its defusedxml equivalent function or make sure defusedxml.defuse_stdlib() is called
issues.judge_reason: xml.etree.ElementTree.fromstring() is used on untrusted XML input. The code does not disable DTD processing or external entity expansion, making it vulnerable to XML bombs and XXE attacks.

duplicate_findings.description: XML parsing depends on a [user-provided value](1) without guarding against uncontrolled entity expansion.
duplicate_findings.judge_reason: Same underlying vulnerability as bandit:B314: ET.fromstring() on user-controlled XML without entity expansion protection. This is a duplicate of the same root cause.
